title: salacia loloensis
intro: Salacia loloensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the "Borneo Cinnamon" due to its spicy and fragrant properties. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the activity of alpha-glucosidase, an enzyme that breaks down carbohydrates into sugars, thereby reducing blood sugar levels. The health benefits of Salacia loloensis include its potential to help manage type 2 diabetes, improve lipid profiles, and aid in weight loss. Salacia loloensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or extract, and is often used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia.
